metadata.title: Full Drift Detection Suite with Evidently + Alibi-Detect + VesselNN

task.title: Add alibi-detect dependency and verify Evidently import
task.description: Add alibi-detect[torch] to the quality optional group in pyproject.toml. Verify evidently (already declared >=0.7) imports correctly. Update __init__.py graceful degradation for alibi-detect.

tests.test: test_evidently_importable — verify evidently can be imported
test_alibi_detect_importable — verify alibi_detect can be imported

task.acceptance: All 4 imports succeed after uv sync --all-extras

task.title: Wire Evidently DataDriftPreset into Tier 1 detector
task.description:
Extend FeatureDriftDetector in src/minivess/observability/drift.py to generate Evidently DataDriftPreset reports alongside existing KS tests. The existing scipy-based KS implementation stays as the statistical backbone; Evidently adds rich HTML reports and Grafana-exportable JSON.
Key: Do NOT replace existing KS tests — Evidently supplements them with visualization and the DataDriftPreset column-level summary.

tests.test: test_evidently_report_contains_all_9_features — all feature columns present in report

task.acceptance: Evidently HTML report generated from volume features, drift detected on synthetic data

task.title: Add Alibi-Detect MMDDrift to Tier 2 detector
task.description:
Extend EmbeddingDriftDetector in src/minivess/observability/drift.py to optionally use alibi_detect.cd.MMDDrift with permutation-based p-values when alibi-detect is available, falling back to the existing sklearn-based MMD implementation when it is not.
Key improvements over existing implementation: - Permutation-based p-values (statistically rigorous) - Configurable kernel (RBF, linear, learned) - Online detection support (for streaming scenarios)

tests.test: test_alibi_mmd_returns_p_value — p_value field populated in DriftResult

task.acceptance: Alibi-Detect MMD test with p-values on synthetic embeddings, graceful fallback

task.title: Register VesselNN as 4th dataset for natural drift simulation
task.description:
Add VesselNN (github.com/petteriTeikari/vesselNN_dataset) to the external dataset registry in src/minivess/data/external_datasets.py. Implement download + format conversion (TIFF to NIfTI if needed). Extract volume features from VesselNN using existing extract_volume_features() and compare against MiniVess reference distribution as a natural drift test.
The 12 VesselNN volumes come from 3 different acquisition campaigns (Burgess 2014, Poon 2015, Santos 2015) with different tissue preparations, creating genuine covariate shift vs. MiniVess data.

tests.test: test_vesselnn_feature_extraction — features extractable from VesselNN format
test_vesselnn_vs_minivess_drift_detected — Tier 1 detects natural covariate shift

task.acceptance: VesselNN features extracted, Tier 1 drift detected vs. MiniVess reference
task.note: Tests that require actual VesselNN download should be marked @pytest.mark.slow and use cached test fixtures for CI

task.title: Persist drift reports as MLflow artifacts
task.description:
Add drift report persistence to MLflow: save Evidently HTML reports, drift detection JSON summaries, and per-feature drift scores as MLflow artifacts under a standardized path convention.
Artifact paths: - drift_reports/tier1_evidently_report.html - drift_reports/tier1_drift_summary.json - drift_reports/tier2_mmd_summary.json - drift_reports/drift_metadata.json (timestamps, thresholds, versions)

tests.test: test_tier1_json_summary_saved — JSON drift summary in artifacts

task.acceptance: Drift reports retrievable from MLflow artifact store after detection run

task.title: Integrate drift detection into Data Flow
task.description:
Add drift detection as a Prefect task in the Data Flow (Flow 1). After data loading and feature extraction, run Tier 1 drift detection comparing current batch features against the reference distribution (stored from previous successful run). If Tier 1 triggers, invoke the existing drift triage agent (data_flow.py:370) with Evidently results.
Flow integration point: 1. data_loading_task → feature_extraction_task → drift_detection_task → triage_drift 2. drift_detection_task saves reports to MLflow (T5) 3. triage_drift recommends action (monitor/investigate/retrain)

tests.test: test_drift_detection_task_exists — @task decorator on drift detection function
test_drift_task_skips_triage_on_negative — triage NOT called when no drift
test_drift_reference_distribution_loaded — reference features loaded from MLflow

task.acceptance: Data Flow runs drift detection post-loading, triggers triage when drift detected

task.title: Integrate embedding drift into Post-Training Flow
task.description:
Add Tier 2 embedding-space drift detection to the Post-Training Flow (Flow 3 / Analysis Flow). After model evaluation, extract penultimate-layer embeddings from the trained model for validation volumes, then run EmbeddingDriftDetector comparing against reference embeddings from the training run.
This catches model-level drift: if the model's learned representations have shifted relative to the reference model, it indicates either data drift impact on training or concept drift.
task.acceptance: Analysis Flow runs embedding drift check, saves Tier 2 report to MLflow

task.title: Add drift summary to Dashboard Flow
task.description:
Extend the Dashboard Flow (Flow 5) to include drift detection summaries in the dashboard JSON output. Pull Tier 1 and Tier 2 drift results from MLflow artifacts and include in the dashboard data section.
Dashboard sections: - drift_tier1: feature-level drift scores, drifted features list - drift_tier2: MMD p-value, embedding drift score - drift_history: temporal trend of drift scores across runs - drift_triage: latest triage recommendation

tests.test: test_dashboard_handles_missing_drift_data — graceful when no drift runs exist

task.acceptance: Dashboard JSON includes drift summaries from both tiers

task.title: E2E integration test: synthetic drift through full pipeline
task.description:
Integration test that exercises the complete drift detection pipeline: 1. Generate reference features from synthetic "clean" volumes 2. Apply parametric drift (intensity shift, noise injection) via drift_synthetic.py 3. Run Tier 1 detection → verify drift detected 4. Generate synthetic embeddings with shift 5. Run Tier 2 detection → verify drift detected 6. Verify reports saved to MLflow artifacts 7. Verify triage agent recommends action
This test uses synthetic data only (no real volumes needed) and is marked @pytest.mark.integration.

tests.test: test_e2e_synthetic_drift_detected_tier1 — full Tier 1 pipeline on synthetic data
test_e2e_synthetic_drift_detected_tier2 — full Tier 2 pipeline on synthetic embeddings
test_e2e_drift_reports_in_mlflow — both tier reports in artifact store
test_e2e_no_false_positive_on_clean_data — no drift on identical distributions

task.acceptance: Full pipeline: inject → detect → report → triage, all assertions pass
